specialism.name: docs-installation
specialism.task: Write or improve a setup or installation page (e.g., docs/src/getting_started.md or docs/src/installation_instructions.md). This page walks a reader from zero to a working Julia environment with the package installed, and optionally covers developer workflows (cloning the repository, running the test suite, building the docs locally). It is almost entirely procedural: every step should be actionable, accurate, and verifiable by the reader without consulting another page. The output is a page a first-time user can follow top-to-bottom and arrive at a working installation.

workflow.step: Gather facts from the project. Read: — Project.toml: the registered package name (for Pkg.add), Julia compat bounds. — test/runtests.jl: whether a test suite exists and what command runs it. — docs/make.jl: whether a docs build exists and what command runs it. These facts determine what procedures to include and what version constraints to state.

Locate the installation page: find docs/src -maxdepth 2 \( -name '*install*' -o -name '*getting_start*' \) Read the current page (if one exists) and identify: missing procedures, inaccurate commands, untagged code fences, prose tips that should be admonitions, and missing H1.

Draft or edit the page following the structure and conventions below.
Canonical structure: 1. H1 = "Installation" or "Getting started" (match whatever the project uses). 2. Opening sentence stating the package is a registered Julia package (if true). 3. H3 for each distinct procedure — do not use H2; the convention is H1 then H3 for all sub-sections. Typical procedures: H3 Basic installation (Pkg.add) H3 Cloning the repository (for contributors) H3 Running the test suite H3 Building the documentation locally 4. Optional steps or alternative paths go inside !!! info admonitions, not inline.
The page is self-contained: avoid @ref cross-references. If linking to another page (e.g., a tutorial), use a relative Markdown path.

Verify accuracy: check that the Pkg.add package name matches the name in Project.toml, that Julia version constraints are consistent with the compat section, and that any test or docs build commands match the current project structure.
Build the docs locally to confirm the page renders without new warnings: julia --project=docs docs/make.jl Resolve any Documenter warnings introduced by the edits before reporting the task complete.

rubric.criterion: All instructions use second-person imperative: "you can install...", "run the following", "press ]". Never passive ("the package can be installed by...") or third-person.
Pkg.add uses the exact registered package name from Project.toml. Julia version bounds match the compat section. Test and build commands match the current project layout.
Sub-sections are H3. The H1 → H3 skip (no H2) is intentional for installation pages and should be preserved even though it violates the general heading-hierarchy convention.
Optional tips, alternative paths, and gotchas appear in !!! info blocks with a quoted friendly title, not as inline paragraphs. Warning-level issues use !!! warning.
The page does not rely on @ref cross-references. A reader who lands directly on this page via a search engine can complete the installation without navigating elsewhere.

formatting.rule: H1 as the page title; all sub-sections as H3 (skipping H2 is intentional here).
Julia Pkg-mode commands use the julia> ] prefix on a separate line: julia> ] pkg> add MyPackage or use the functional form inside a julia block: `Pkg.add("MyPackage")`.
Shell commands go in a sh block; Julia commands in a julia block. Never leave a fence untagged.
!!! info "Friendly title" for optional tips and alternative paths. !!! warning "Title" for real gotchas (e.g., version incompatibilities).
No @ref links. If pointing to another docs page, use a relative Markdown path.
No badges, no embedded images, no figures.
formatting.example:
## Before (missing H1, untagged fence, tip buried in prose)
MyPackage is a registered Julia package. To install:
``` using Pkg; Pkg.add("MyPackage") ```
If you want to run the tests you can do `Pkg.test("MyPackage")`. Note: you need Julia 1.9 or higher.
## After (H1, tagged fence, H3 sub-sections, !!! info admonition)
# Installation
MyPackage is a registered Julia package. Install it from the Julia package manager:
```julia using Pkg Pkg.add("MyPackage") ```
!!! info "Julia version requirement" MyPackage requires Julia 1.9 or higher. Check your version with `julia --version`.
### Running the test suite
To verify your installation, run the package tests:
```julia using Pkg Pkg.test("MyPackage") ```
### Building the documentation locally
```sh julia --project=docs docs/make.jl ```
